---
title: "Week 4: Lexicon-Based Sentiment Analysis"
subtitle: "EDS 231 — Spring 2026"
date: "April 14, 2026"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: bootstrap
    highlight: pygments
    css: week-styles.css
    toc: false
    toc_float:
      collapsed: false
      smooth_scroll: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

> Lexicon-based sentiment analysis applied to Bluesky social media data.

---

### Learning Objectives

By the end of this week, you will be able to:

 - Authenticate with a social media API and retrieve posts by keyword using `{atrrr}`
 - Clean and tokenize social media text for downstream sentiment analysis
 - Apply and interpret multiple lexicon-based sentiment methods
 - Identify common failure modes of general-purpose lexicons on domain-specific environmental text
 - Compare method outputs using correlation matrices and distribution visualizations
 - Evaluate which method is most appropriate for a given corpus and research question

---

### Recommended Reading

📖  [SMLTR Ch. 2 — Sentiment Analysis](https://smltar.com/sentiment)

📖  [Text Mining with R — Ch. 2: Sentiment Analysis with Tidy Data](https://www.tidytextmining.com/sentiment)

---

## Lecture Materials

### Slides

🖥 Slide deck from lecture

[View Slides](https://ucsb.box.com/s/lrppb4ei4o5fdq0xtstumx891yit7fje)

### Code & Data

💻 R scripts used during class:

[Lab 3 Markdown](https://github.com/MaRo406/EDS-231-text-sentiment/blob/main/Labs/Lab3.Rmd)

---

### 📦 Packages

New packages introduced this week:

| Package | Purpose | Install |
|---|---|---|
| `atrrr` | Bluesky (AT Protocol) API client — search posts, authenticate, retrieve feeds | `install.packages("atrrr")` |
| `sentimentr` | Sentence-level sentiment scoring with valence shifters (negation, amplifiers) | `install.packages("sentimentr")` |
| `tidyvader` | Tidy interface to the VADER sentiment lexicon, optimized for social media text | `remotes::install_github("chris31415926535/tidyvader")` |

---

## 📂 Assignments

### Lab 3 — Lexicon-Based Sentiment Analysis with Bluesky Data <span class="badge badge-danger">Graded · 11 pts</span>

**Due: April 28 @ 11:59 pm**

Complete the assignment laid out in the Lab 3 markdown. Submit your .Rmd and knitted HTML to Canvas. Make sure your Bluesky credentials are stored in `.Renviron` and do not appear anywhere in your submitted file.  Look your knitted product over before submission to make sure all plots rendered and extraneous print-outs did not render.

---
